Hello! In recent days, some drivers have complained about fines that were allegedly issued thanks to a new monitoring system used by the Municipal Civil Guard.  In this specific case, a totem installed in Maria Corina Square, in Barra do Imbuí, right?  We recently showcased this device here, which has 360º cameras, facial recognition, and other features.  And it is located exactly at the intersection of Dr. Oliveira Street and Manuel Dias Street.  The problem, according to the drivers there, is that there's a junction at that location, and currently the signage isn't 100% adequate.  Look at that little clover over there, it's striped, it's not really a clover, is it?  A painting called " pipeline painting," right?  A lane marking, a striped or zebra-striped lane with three or four studs indicating that those in the right lane can only proceed onto Dr. Oliveira Street in Barra do Imbuí.  Those heading towards the city center need to move to the left lane.  However, since the much larger flow of vehicles coming from Presidente Rosville enters Manuel Dias, including vehicles coming from Petrópolis to access Teresópolis, they continue towards the city center.  Drivers sometimes end up going straight through the intersection, and they are receiving fines thanks to the analysis from the cameras of that equipment there in Barra.  Well, we contacted the guard and are still waiting for an official statement, a note, but the unofficial information is that the fines that were applied in this situation were only four fines, and very high fines at that, okay?  That's a very serious infraction, according to the Traffic Code, article 193, right?  The fine is R$ 293, but in this case it is multiplied by 3, resulting in R$880 and 7 points on the driver's license.  However, unofficial information suggests that the four fines issued in this situation were not generated by the use of cameras, but rather by a traffic officer present at the location. We are also investigating situations involving mainly motorcyclists who come from Dr. Oliveira and, in order to avoid going around Manuel Dias and then entering Presidente Roosevelt, pass straight through where that little yellow and black structure is located. The motorcycle was being used to cut across a path to reach President Rusville, which is also prohibited.  We'll return to this topic shortly.  I briefly mentioned yesterday about a scam involving the sale of a property, an apartment here in the city.  And to warn the public, you know, to give tips on how not to suffer losses in this type of situation, right?  Besides, the apartment costs a lot of money, doesn't it?  We spoke with a real estate agent. We invited Rafael Zinco here, who is a real estate agent, because , Rafael, we have a situation that has been happening a lot in the municipality. Teresópolis has been receiving many people who want to live here in the city, and the real estate market is booming.  And so , the scammers, obviously seeing an opportunity to commit fraud, begin to use this important sector of the municipality's economy to scam people who intend to buy or rent a property. A case of suspected fraud in the sale of an apartment in Teresópolis is being investigated by the 11th police station.  A couple claims to have bought the property for R$ 190,000, while the owner says she never made the sale.  According to the police report, the couple tried to take possession of the apartment, but were prevented by the building manager, who called the Military Police and the owner of the property.  The owner presented documents proving she was the rightful owner.  The case continues by invitation.  We are from Velite Barbosa, we have been in the market for 19 years and we came here because of a scam that happened yesterday in Teresópolis. The person even went to the police station where they bought a property that was supposedly through Caixa Econômica (a Brazilian bank), but upon arriving there, they fell victim to a scam.  And that's why I always tell our clients that you need to be very well advised.  And today, with the city's growth, scams are more likely to emerge.  So what's my advice? First, let's remove one UN, okay?  Today you can obtain a ONU, which is the property registration certificate, on a website called Registrares, which is issued immediately and costs R$ 52. It will show the owner's name, and then you will request the owner's ID to verify if that person is indeed the owner, if they have the power to sell the property, and then automatically obtain all the necessary certificates: civil, tax, labor, legal, guardianship, and whenever you are with a real estate agent, the agent should present the CRES (Regional Council of Real Estate Brokers) registration number so that the person can access the CRES website and verify if that person is duly registered with the institution. Now, this is the type of scam for those who intend to acquire, to buy the property, but there are also scams for those who want to rent a property, right, Rafael?  Yes, I've seen rental scams where someone rents a property, supposedly for R$2,000, goes to the real estate agency, gets the keys, makes a copy, takes a photo, advertises it on LX, and states that the property is being rented for R$1,000 for viewing with that person.  That person goes there, shows you the property, you'll see that the property actually exists, and they'll say: "Look, there are a lot of people in line here, I need to close this deal today, and I need three months' deposit."  The naive person goes there, deposits the R$ 3,000, that contract that doesn't actually exist, and when they arrive at the property, they're kicked out because that person, who wasn't a real estate agent, much less the owner, committed this scam.  Now, Rafael, it's very important to point out that many of these scams happen because the person ends up wanting a price far below the market value, right? Exactly.  I think people even ask for it, right?  Even when those Instagram scams started showing up, offering a TV that costs R$ 4,000 for R$ 1,000 and getting an early Pix payment, it seems like she already wanted that .  If the property is advertised for 2000, you can see that there are difficulties in renting it. And that same property is advertised for R$ 1,000, something's up. Exactly. The topic now is flu vaccination, right? Maria Eduarda Maia is the one who has the information . With winter and the drop in temperatures, concern also increases regarding respiratory illnesses, especially the flu, which usually sees more cases at this time of year. Therefore, experts emphasize that the flu vaccine remains the primary measure to prevent complications from this disease.  And in Teresópolis, a municipality known for its colder climate, the Municipal Health Department has intensified its warning to the entire population to keep their vaccination records up to date.  This winter period, with the cold weather, right? In our municipality specifically, it's very cold, which facilitates the transmission of the influenza virus, which is what the vaccine protects against.  So the importance of this is that people who get vaccinated will be protecting themselves from complications caused by this virus.  So, the elderly, pregnant women, and children, who are the most vulnerable groups, as well as people with comorbidities, are our priority target audience because they are more susceptible to complications from pneumonia and even death due to respiratory illnesses.  So it's very important to get vaccinated now during this period to protect ourselves from complications, right?  The priority group , we really need them to seek out the vaccination centers because, as I said, this is the group that experiences the most complications and is most likely to develop pneumonia from the virus.  So, and this is the group that sometimes searches the least.  So we need this group—the elderly, pregnant women, children under 6 years old, and people with comorbidities—to get vaccinated to avoid these complications, to avoid pneumonia, these respiratory illnesses in general, and even deaths, right?  So we need to increase coverage of this specific group a little more .  The group that really needs it is still too small.  So what we need is for this population to seek out the vaccine, and we have been facilitating access. Urban mobility is one of the major challenges faced by Teresópolis. In recent years, the city has seen its vehicle fleet grow significantly, while the streets and traffic control system have remained virtually unchanged. The result is felt daily by those who have to deal with traffic jams, especially during peak hours.  Given this scenario, the city government sees the modernization of the traffic light system as one of the strategies to improve traffic flow, as highlighted by the Secretary of Security, Public Order and Mobility, Sérgio Mauro. A city that has a traffic light system from 20 years ago, where the municipal guard has to climb from pole to pole.  And we are holding a bidding process to install a digital traffic light system, digitized, with artificial intelligence monitoring, which calculates the time and sees the number of cars.  So, studies are being done, but what we have today is done analogically; you have to climb from pole to pole. So it really happens, look, you have to synchronize all the signals, okay, every time there's a power surge, every time something changes, right, it has to pick up the signal, go from pole to pole. So, there are times when traffic is heavier, and if I want to change the traffic light timing, it will have to go up. So, the city is way behind in this and changes need to happen. The expectation is that the modernization will begin to move from the planning stage to implementation in the coming days.  The bidding process is already scheduled, and according to Sérgio Mauro, the new system will allow for remote monitoring of traffic lights and will be beneficial in various situations in the city, including, for example, better integration between intersections, allowing for more efficient synchronization of signal timing according to vehicle flow. We are working towards that.  In fact, on July 5th there will be a bidding process for the traffic light system which, God willing, will be entirely digital.  We won't need to climb poles anymore.  It will be monitored 24 hours a day using artificial intelligence.  We can operate it inside a room.  M.
